architecture.summary: The system augments a Swin Transformer backbone with a neurosymbolic layer that combines focal set reasoning and differentiable fuzzy logic. The transformer encodes images into an embedding space where data-driven focal sets are induced to capture epistemic uncertainty over multiple plausible fine-grained classes. A belief-theoretic layer applies fuzzy membership functions and t-norm conjunctions to enforce logical consistency between fine- and coarse-grained hierarchical predictions. A learnable composite loss balances calibration, mass regularization, and logical consistency, allowing the model to adaptively trade off symbolic structure against data-driven evidence.

biblio.title: A neurosymbolic Approach with Epistemic Deep Learning for Hierarchical Image Classification

claims.statement: the framework maintains accuracy on par with transformer baselines while providing more calibrated and interpretable predictions
focal sets induce epistemic uncertainty over multiple plausible fine-grained classes within the learnt embedding space
fuzzy membership functions and t-norm conjunctions encourage consistency between fine- and coarse-grained predictions
the framework reduces overconfidence in predictions
the framework enforces high logical consistency across hierarchical outputs
deep neural networks often produce overconfident predictions as which fail to express epistemic uncertainty, and frequently violate logical or structural constraints present in the data
